name: Travel & Landmarks
description: Explore the world's most iconic destinations and landmarks.

questions.text: In which country is the Eiffel Tower located?
questions.explanation: The Eiffel Tower is located in Paris, France, and was constructed in 1889 for the World's Fair.

questions.text: What is the capital city of Australia?
questions.explanation: Canberra is the capital of Australia, purpose-built as a compromise between rival cities Sydney and Melbourne.

questions.text: On which continent is the Sahara Desert located?
questions.explanation: The Sahara Desert is located in North Africa and is the world's largest hot desert.

questions.text: Which city is known as the 'City of Canals'?
questions.explanation: Venice, Italy is famously known as the City of Canals, with its historic streets replaced by waterways.

questions.text: The Great Wall of China was primarily built to protect against invasions from which direction?
questions.explanation: The Great Wall of China was built along the northern border to protect against nomadic invasions from the north.

questions.text: In which country is Machu Picchu located?
questions.explanation: Machu Picchu is an ancient Inca citadel located in the Andes Mountains of Peru.

questions.text: Which ocean is the largest in the world?
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean is the world's largest ocean, covering approximately 165 million square kilometers.

questions.text: What is the tallest mountain in the world?
questions.explanation: Mount Everest, located in the Himalayas on the Nepal-Tibet border, is the world's tallest mountain at 8,849 meters.

questions.text: Which city is home to the Colosseum?
questions.explanation: The Colosseum (Flavian Amphitheatre) is located in Rome, Italy, and was completed in 80 AD.

questions.text: The Statue of Liberty was a gift to the USA from which country?
questions.explanation: The Statue of Liberty was a gift of friendship from France to the United States, dedicated in 1886.

questions.text: Which river is the longest in the world?
questions.explanation: The Nile River in Africa is traditionally recognized as the world's longest river at approximately 6,650 kilometers.

questions.text: Which country has the most UNESCO World Heritage Sites?
questions.explanation: Italy has the most UNESCO World Heritage Sites, with 58 listed sites as of 2023.

questions.text: What is the name of the famous clock tower in London?
questions.explanation: The tower is officially called Elizabeth Tower; Big Ben is actually the nickname of the main bell inside it.

questions.text: In which country is the Acropolis located?
questions.explanation: The Acropolis of Athens is located in Greece and is home to the Parthenon, built in the 5th century BC.

questions.text: Which city is known as 'The Big Apple'?
questions.explanation: New York City is nicknamed 'The Big Apple', a term popularized in the 1970s by the city's tourism campaign.

questions.text: What type of transportation is a 'gondola' associated with in Venice?
questions.explanation: A gondola is a traditional flat-bottomed rowing boat used on the canals of Venice, propelled by a gondolier.

questions.text: The Taj Mahal, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, is located in which Indian city?
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal is located in Agra, India, and was built by Emperor Shah Jahan as a mausoleum for his wife Mumtaz Mahal.

questions.text: Which country is the only one to span two continents and is often called the crossroads of Europe and Asia?
questions.explanation: Turkey (Türkiye) spans both Europe and Asia, with Istanbul straddling the Bosphorus Strait dividing the two continents.

questions.text: What is the world's smallest country by area?
questions.explanation: Vatican City covers approximately 0.44 km² within Rome, making it the world's smallest country.

questions.text: The northern lights (Aurora Borealis) are best viewed in which of these countries?
questions.explanation: Iceland is one of the best places to see the Northern Lights due to its location just below the Arctic Circle and low light pollution.

questions.text: Which ancient wonder of the world still exists today?
questions.explanation: The Great Pyramid of Giza is the only ancient wonder of the world still largely intact today.

questions.text: Which US state contains the most active volcanoes?
questions.explanation: Alaska has more active volcanoes than any other US state, with over 50 volcanoes that have been active since 1760.

questions.text: Angkor Wat, the world's largest religious monument, is located in which country?
questions.explanation: Angkor Wat is a Hindu-Buddhist temple complex in Siem Reap, Cambodia, built in the 12th century.

questions.text: What is the name of the world's largest salt flat, located in Bolivia?
questions.explanation: Salar de Uyuni in Bolivia is the world's largest salt flat, covering over 10,000 km² and containing large lithium reserves.

questions.text: In which year did the Channel Tunnel (Eurotunnel) linking England and France open?
questions.explanation: The Channel Tunnel opened to the public in November 1994, connecting Folkestone in England to Coquelles near Calais in France.

questions.text: The ancient city of Petra is carved into rose-red cliffs in which country?
questions.explanation: Petra is an ancient city in Jordan carved into rose-red sandstone cliffs and was the capital of the Nabataean Kingdom.

questions.text: Which airline is the flag carrier of Singapore?
questions.explanation: Singapore Airlines is the flag carrier of Singapore and is consistently ranked among the world's best airlines.

questions.text: What is the name of the famous thermal baths complex in Budapest, Hungary?
options: All of the above are famous Budapest baths
questions.explanation: Budapest has several famous thermal baths including Széchenyi, Gellért, and Rudas, all fed by natural hot springs.

questions.text: The Inca Trail leading to Machu Picchu is approximately how long?
questions.explanation: The classic Inca Trail is approximately 43 km (26 miles) long and typically takes 4 days to hike.

questions.text: Which city is home to the world's busiest airport by passenger traffic (as of recent years)?
questions.explanation: Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport has historically been the world's busiest airport by passenger traffic.

questions.text: The famous Zhangjiajie National Forest in China, known for its towering sandstone pillars, inspired the setting of which film?
questions.explanation: James Cameron cited Zhangjiajie's floating sandstone pillars as a visual inspiration for the Hallelujah Mountains in Avatar (2009).

questions.text: What is the maximum altitude of the Burj Khalifa in Dubai?
questions.explanation: The Burj Khalifa stands at 828 meters (2,717 feet), making it the tallest building in the world.

questions.text: Which Scandinavian country contains the most fjords?
questions.explanation: Norway has the highest concentration of fjords in the world; its coastline is dotted with thousands of them.

questions.text: Hagia Sophia, originally built as a Christian cathedral, is located in which city?
questions.explanation: Hagia Sophia is located in Istanbul, Turkey; built in 537 AD as a Byzantine cathedral, it later became a mosque, then a museum, and mosque again.

questions.text: Which is the only country through which both the equator and the Tropic of Capricorn pass?
questions.explanation: Brazil is the only country through which both the equator and the Tropic of Capricorn pass.

questions.text: The Camino de Santiago pilgrimage route traditionally ends at which city in Spain?
questions.explanation: The Camino de Santiago ends at the Cathedral of Santiago de Compostela in Galicia, Spain, believed to hold the remains of St. James.

questions.text: What is the deepest lake in the world?
questions.explanation: Lake Baikal in Siberia, Russia, is the world's deepest lake at 1,642 meters and contains about 20% of the world's fresh surface water.

questions.text: The Pergamon Museum, housing reconstructed ancient monuments including the Ishtar Gate, is located in which city?
questions.explanation: The Pergamon Museum in Berlin, Germany, houses the reconstructed Ishtar Gate of Babylon and other ancient monuments.

questions.text: The ancient trading city of Samarkand, a major stop on the Silk Road, is located in which modern country?
questions.explanation: Samarkand is located in modern-day Uzbekistan and was one of the most important cities on the ancient Silk Road.

questions.text: What is the name of the phenomenon where travelers visit destinations to see them before they disappear due to climate change?
questions.explanation: Last-chance tourism (also called doom tourism) describes traveling to destinations under threat of destruction from climate change or other forces.

questions.text: The Trolltunga rock formation, a popular tourist site projecting horizontally over a fjord, is located in which country?
questions.explanation: Trolltunga (Troll's Tongue) is a rock formation projecting horizontally over Lake Ringedalsvatnet in Hardanger, Norway.

questions.text: Which UNESCO-listed site is located in the middle of the Libyan Desert and contains prehistoric rock art?
questions.explanation: Tassili n'Ajjer in Algeria is a UNESCO World Heritage Site containing over 15,000 petroglyphs and cave paintings from prehistoric times.

questions.text: What is the world's longest railway line, spanning from Moscow to Vladivostok?
questions.explanation: The Trans-Siberian Railway stretches approximately 9,289 km from Moscow to Vladivostok, making it the longest railway line in the world.

questions.text: The ancient city of Çatalhöyük, one of the world's earliest urban settlements, is located in which modern country?
questions.explanation: Çatalhöyük is a Neolithic settlement in south-central Turkey, dating back to approximately 7500 BC and a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.text: Which country is home to Socotra Island, known for its alien-like dragon blood trees?
questions.explanation: Socotra Island belongs to Yemen and is a UNESCO World Heritage Site famous for its unique dragon blood trees and high level of endemic species.

questions.text: The Nazca Lines, large geoglyphs visible only from the air, are located in which South American country?
questions.explanation: The Nazca Lines are ancient geoglyphs in the Nazca Desert of southern Peru, designated a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.text: Which is the highest navigable lake in the world?
questions.explanation: Lake Titicaca on the Bolivia-Peru border is the world's highest navigable lake, sitting at an elevation of 3,812 meters above sea level.

questions.text: The Alhambra palace complex, a masterpiece of Moorish architecture, is located in which Spanish city?
questions.explanation: The Alhambra is a palace and fortress complex in Granada, Andalusia, Spain, built in the 13th and 14th centuries by Moorish rulers.

questions.text: What is the name of the geological phenomenon at Pamukkale in Turkey, which creates white terraced pools?
questions.explanation: Pamukkale's dazzling white terraces are made of travertine, a sedimentary rock deposited by carbonate-rich hot spring waters over millennia.

questions.text: The Wieliczka Salt Mine, a UNESCO World Heritage Site with underground chapels, is located in which country?
questions.explanation: The Wieliczka Salt Mine near Kraków, Poland, has been mined since the 13th century and features underground chapels, sculptures, and lakes carved entirely from salt.
